headline: Cosmetic Lens Maker Moody Considers Sale That Could Value Company at $1 Billion
summary: Shanghai Moody Technology is working with an adviser and may sound out buyers months after raising over 1 billion yuan from investors.

headline: KKR Insider Buying And New Retail Fund Reshape Growth Story
summary: KKR (NYSE:KKR) Co CEOs and an independent board director recently made significant insider purchases of KKR shares, increasing their personal stakes in the firm. The company has also launched its first private equity fund for individual investors in partnership with Capital Group, expanding access beyond traditional institutional clients. KKR, a global investment firm known for its private equity, credit, and real assets platforms, sits at the center of long running interest in alternative...

headline: Oaktree's Howard Marks says there's no systemic problem with private credit
summary: Marks said any risk stems from the pace of expansion in direct lending, which has ballooned to a market now exceeding $1 trillion since inception around 2011.

raw.headline: Oaktree's Howard Marks says there's no systemic problem with private credit

headline: South Korea Private Equity Deal Activity Falls In 2025
summary: Global private equity and venture capital deal value in South Korea totaled $8.58B, down 38.8% from prior year and the lowest annual figure since at least 2020.

raw.headline: South Korea Private Equity Deal Activity Falls In 2025
raw.summary: Global private equity and venture capital deal value in South Korea totaled $8.58B, down 38.8% from prior year and the lowest annual figure since at least 2020.

headline: How The Story Is Shifting For KKR (KKR) As Analysts Rework Valuation Targets
summary: The average fair value estimate for KKR has been revised from US$143.87 to US$140.40 per share, signaling a modest reset in where analysts currently see equilibrium value. That adjustment sits alongside a wave of fresh initiations, rating changes, and target cuts to levels like US$127, US$125 and US$168, as well as at least one move to Hold, showing how split the Street is between long term franchise views and nearer term execution risk. As you read on, you will see how these shifting targets...
